topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the apartment crackled with anticipation. Sarah bounced on the balls of her feet, a wide, unrestrained grin stretching across her face. Balloons, shimmering in every color imaginable, dotted the living room, and the playlist was set to an upbeat, celebratory volume. "They're going to be incredible," she murmured to herself, running a hand through her already messy hair. She checked her phone again, a flicker of hope sparking in her chest. The RSVP list was confirmed, the caterer had confirmed, and the venue was locked in. Then, the email landed. A single, stark sentence. Sarah read it twice, her smile slowly freezing, the bright energy draining away until she felt a strange, hollow ache settle in her stomach.

[story 2]

Mark let out a whoop, throwing his hands up in the air as confetti rained down from the ceiling. He was practically vibrating with excitement, his eyes sparkling with pure delight as he surveyed the meticulously decorated backyard. "This is going to be epic!" he shouted, his voice booming with uncontainable joy. He was just about to start the main event, to raise a glass and toast the birthday person, when his phone buzzed. He picked it up, and the words swam into focus. The cancellation notice. Mark blinked, the vibrant color draining from his cheeks. His raised arms slowly lowered, his posture deflating as a heavy silence descended, leaving him staring blankly at the suddenly still decorations.

[story 3]

"I can't believe it!" Chloe practically sang, spinning around to admire the elaborate setup. Her energy was boundless; she couldn't sit still, pacing the perimeter of the room, her laughter bubbling up in quick, bright bursts. Every detail was perfect—the lighting, the food arrangement, the carefully chosen music. She was practically buzzing with readiness for the evening. But when she saw the notification pop up on her screen, the sound of her own excitement seemed to catch in her throat. Her rapid movements ceased instantly. She stood perfectly still, her breath catching unevenly, a sudden, profound stillness replacing the frantic energy that had filled her moments before.
